dimensions.name: Research rigor and synthesis
dimensions.definition: Quality, authority, currency, cross-checking, synthesis, and decision impact of the research.

anchors.1: Superficial or uncited research, fabricated sources, little domain understanding, or no connection to decisions.
anchors.5: Credible coverage of users, competitors, workflows, and conventions, but synthesis or decision impact is uneven.
anchors.10: Authoritative, current, cross-checked research exposes a real opportunity and directly shapes positioning, requirements, information architecture, and execution.

dimensions.name: Product strategy, PRD, and GTM readiness
dimensions.definition: Focus, differentiation, requirements, security/data assumptions, measurable success, and buyer-to-retention plan.

anchors.1: Vague audience, generic platform scope, missing requirements, and no credible adoption or commercial path.
anchors.5: A plausible product and PRD with users, workflows, requirements, and a basic GTM story, but meaningful gaps or contradictions remain.
anchors.10: Focused differentiated thesis, precise requirements and non-goals, credible security/data assumptions, measurable success, and a coherent buyer-to-activation-to-retention plan.

dimensions.name: Design specification and artifact coherence
dimensions.definition: Completeness and decisiveness of IA, flows, states, visualization behavior, accessibility, constraints, and implementation fidelity.

anchors.1: Design notes are absent, generic, or disconnected from the PRD and implementation.
anchors.5: Usable IA, flows, responsive notes, and visual direction, with some under-specified states or implementation drift.
anchors.10: Complete and decisive specification anticipates journeys, states, visualization behavior, accessibility, and constraints; the implementation clearly realizes it.

dimensions.name: Cybersecurity domain fitness and workflow quality
dimensions.definition: Operational credibility, prioritization, explainability, collaboration, remediation, trust, and auditability of the security workflow.

anchors.1: Misleading metrics, implausible workflows, unsafe assumptions, or a decorative dashboard with no operational value.
anchors.5: Credible terminology and a useful core workflow, but prioritization, explainability, collaboration, remediation, or trust is incomplete.
anchors.10: The product helps its chosen user make defensible security decisions through excellent prioritization, context, evidence, action, permissions, and auditability.

dimensions.name: Information design and visual execution
dimensions.definition: Operational clarity, hierarchy, density, honest encoding of risk and uncertainty, visual differentiation, and production quality.

anchors.1: Unreadable density, arbitrary charts/colors, weak hierarchy, or default-looking cards that obscure important signals.
anchors.5: Professional and usable with mostly appropriate visualization, but hierarchy, density, or visual differentiation is inconsistent.
anchors.10: Exceptional operational clarity: data, risk, uncertainty, trends, and actions are encoded honestly and scanned effortlessly in a distinctive, production-quality system.

dimensions.name: Frontend craft, interaction, and responsiveness
dimensions.definition: Behavior, persistence, feedback, state coverage, semantic accessibility, responsiveness, and polish of the frontend demo.

anchors.1: Static mock, broken controls, console errors, poor mobile behavior, inaccessible interaction, or unfinished states.
anchors.5: Core interactions work and the demo is responsive, with some shallow behavior, missing states, or visible roughness.
anchors.10: Convincing product behavior, purposeful persistence, excellent feedback and state coverage, semantic accessibility, and polished desktop and mobile execution without relying on a backend.
